name: Green Chile Stew
recipeIngredient: 1 pound lean pork, cut into 1-inch cubes
Hot green chiles to taste – see note
recipeInstructions:
1. Roast the chilies, peel them, remove seeds, and cut into coarse chunks (Note).
2. Heat a Dutch oven or medium saucepan over high heat.
3. Saute onion, garlic, oregano, and cumin until onion is clear.
4. Add green chiles and saute, stirring to coat the pork cubes on all sides.
5. Add pork cubes and stir to sear all sides; add lime juice and mix.
6. Add chicken stock, ensuring most of the pork cubes are covered with liquid.
7. Stir well, reduce heat to a simmer, cover, and set timer for 30 minutes.
8. Check occasionally to ensure it isn't scorching on the bottom.
9. When the timer goes off, check consistency; add more stock if too thick/dry or cook uncovered to thicken if too runny.
10. Add salt and black pepper.
11. Serve with fresh corn tortillas, a pepper-garlic-onion garnish, and cold beer, horchata, or jamaica.
Notes:
- Long green chiles: If fresh unavailable, use canned; taste will differ slightly due to added citric acid.
- Hot chiles: Anaheim chiles are mild. Some prefer jalapenos; serranos provide more heat. Adjust quantity based on preference.
- Garnish options include chopped fresh chiles (ancho or poblano), onion, garlic, olive oil, and dried oregano. Fresh cilantro or epazote can also be added for flavor.
